_README: Graph hooks for code-review-graph + graphify. Commit this as .claude/settings.example.json; each teammate copies it to .claude/settings.local.json (or ~/.claude/settings.json for all repos). Every hook COMMAND is a stable thin wrapper that resolves its script repo-first then home ($CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/scripts then $HOME/.claude/scripts) and runs it. Two reasons: (1) repo-first resolution means a repo-local copy WINS over the home copy; (2) the wrapper text never changes across versions, so Claude Code's native de-duplication (identical command string + args fire once) collapses a home install and a repo install into a SINGLE fire instead of double-running. Every hook silently no-ops when CRG/graphify is not installed or no graph exists. graphify is intentionally NOT in any Claude hook (too slow ~10s; runs only via git hooks).

hooks.command: R="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || echo "$PWD")}"; H="$R/.graph-hooks/hook.sh"; [ -f "$H" ] || H="$HOME/.graph-hooks/hook.sh"; [ -f "$H" ] && bash "$H" --tool claude --kind pretool-shell || true

R="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || echo "$PWD")}"; H="$R/.graph-hooks/hook.sh"; [ -f "$H" ] || H="$HOME/.graph-hooks/hook.sh"; [ -f "$H" ] && bash "$H" --tool claude --kind pretool-read || true

R="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || echo "$PWD")}"; H="$R/.graph-hooks/hook.sh"; [ -f "$H" ] || H="$HOME/.graph-hooks/hook.sh"; [ -f "$H" ] && bash "$H" --tool claude --kind sessionstart || true

R="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || echo "$PWD")}"; H="$R/.graph-hooks/hook.sh"; [ -f "$H" ] || H="$HOME/.graph-hooks/hook.sh"; [ -f "$H" ] && bash "$H" --tool claude --kind endturn || true
